review_title: We start using clustercontrol and stopped looking for alternatives
description: We are now able to scale up to meet user demand as well as administer our databases from a single point.
author: Musa M.
pros: Ease of use is one. We love the features and how it brings some other related services under one administrative control e.g. haproxy. Reports in their various forms and the ability to create custom reports are some of the features we're most in love with as well. Clustercontrols's quick provisioning and decommissioning is a right fit for our scaling needs.
cons: Customer support. For some reason I am not able to reach customer support via email and for that reason we're stuck using the community edition with reduced features at this time.

review_title: enjoying using this software since it is very helpful and interactive software
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: i like cluster control due to its ability to centralize the communication from both analog and digital I/O . Monitoring system activities is easy Eg managing data record on a large scale like data for organization which could have been tedious to handle.
cons: involves keen and logical thinking and has got limitations in diffrent environments in which the software is working for example When using windows CE. when a control overlaps the cluster shell in a Touch Panel VI, the entire control is still completely visible when you build the VI into an application. If the control is completely outside the cluster shell, the control will not be not visible.

review_title: Its good, still not fulfil some functionality which required for big production.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Its really did many important tasks efficiently. I liked the auto recovery option, backup verification, Restore option, auto upload to cloud. Migration processes very smooth.
cons: 60% ability to handle big production system. The backup operation still has many missing issues which we need to handle big database. Job Schedule functionality is a single thread. Graphs and Optimisation are not perfect for high load environment. Don't have checkup option. I found some major bug like add a new node which is not cloning my my.cnf configuration as like others nodes hand. CC generate a default configuration which had a bug and I was not able to deploy a new node. Without test related this type of updates is very unexpected.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: very good cross platform tool
description: maybe this is the best solution, even community version, for DB tuning!
author: Alexander B.
pros: very good dashboards, you can find weak places on comparing dashboards values in one place. Maybe the best solution for tuning databases values.
cons: 1. you couldn't skip some alerts. For example, you have alert from category DBHealth about Use wsrep_sst_method=xtrabackup-v2 instead. But I don't like to use it and I don't like to skip all the warnings of DBHealth category . 2. It could be better if there was a line, across all dashboards. So for example, you put your mouse point on one place of dashboard and the line shows the statement on other - this would be really good. 3. Not good, that you don't show prices for commercial solutions( But You have made something really cool
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: ClusterControl is an all in one database management platform with a tons of features
author: Mohammed F.
pros: We have tested the cluster control tool trial. It's loaded with tons of database management features and it is reliable. Some of the key features of this tool is as follows. Easy to deploy and manage production database systems. Advanced Auto recovery during node failures. Database servers can be scaled and managed easily. Multi node cluster support. Galera support for MySQL. MongoDB replication and Sharding clusters. Scheduled auto backup. Advanced database monitoring with integrated tools to gain insight from metrics. Excellent support team.
cons: Need to include features for managing other DBMS and NoSQL database like Hbase etc.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Not fully tested
author: Stephen L.
pros: I loved the trial I had of cluster control. It provides an aws rds type layer on top of a galera cluster. It also promises cluster recovery in the event of crashed nodes.
cons: I didn't get a chance to fully test all of the features so I can't comment as to the reliability of all the promised functionality.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: A must have for database management
description: I use ClusterControl to deploy and manage galera cluster of my clients. Saved so much time deploying and monitoring clusters with CC.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use. You can create a cluster and monitor your database with ease ! No need to worry about boring configuration, CC do the work.
cons: Price per node is a little bit to expensive but worth it

review_title: My Experience about cluster control
author: Kamal R.
pros: - Ease of installation - Good documentation - Ease of cluster recovery - Very good GUI - Very good monitoring features
cons: - Community version don't have essential features like adding new nodes
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great product, easy to use
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's rich features such as mysql failover, alerts and high availability. Moreover roles of users foynd to be useful too.
cons: Price is quite high knowing that it works on open source technologies. Price model could be reviewed.

review_title: My honest review of CC 1.7.1
author: Raul G.
pros: Very simple to operate Intuitive Great support
cons: Nothing significant comes to my mind, no cons yet

review_title: Great software for managing databases
description: So far we have only done an initial evaluation of ClusterControl and found it to be very good, but too much is changing with our backend systems right now to move forward with any possible purchase decisions. That being said we definitely see where it can solve some of our biggest problems that we have encountered with our MySQL database.
author: Jeffrey P.
pros: Makes many tasks that are typically very difficult very easy like monitoring, automatically failing over in case of a server problem, building new database servers and managing replication.
cons: It would be nice to have tighter integration with MaxScale to have more options for sql aware proxies.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: The quickest way to deploy a good Cluster in a few steps.
description: I start to use it without know any about this type of clusters, thanks to this I setted up my first MySQL Cluster and it introduce me to this scope.
author: Javier F.
pros: The ease to set up it and start to use it. If you haven't deploy any Cluster before, this is the better way to do it.

review_title: Amazed.
description: Ease of use. Being able to have my Kubernetes Galera clúster auto discovered. Graphs and insight to my cluster.
author: Juan V.
pros: I love how intuitive the interface is. Also, how easy it is to move around and find the things I need. Love the product.

review_title: Great and easy to use software
description: We're using ClusterControl to manage our Galera Cluster, or better, ClusterConrtol cares about our Galera Cluster so we can focus on our main tasks.

pros: ItÄs easy to configure and once setup, you dont need to care about it. Everything will be done automatically without deep knowledege for MySQL / Galera Clusters.

review_title: Amazing! Just the price friendly system we looked for.

cons: I don`t have much bad to say, I just like it like this. Probably its a bit difficult to handle as an beginner but due to the great bandwith of their wiki you are able to learn fast.

review_title: good tool
author: bala K.
pros: This is a very good tool to manage cluster from GUI. deploying nodes and monitoring performance is very good. user flexibility is also very good.
cons: Adding some extra hints for every option will help for the users
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: King of the Database Clusterization
description: Installing and configuring steps need some expert administration.. Because wrong settings may occured to lost your databases.. But with some calm, a successful installation can be made with patient and careful work. After installation everything was perfect.. Query monitor is also perfect for missing database settings.. Our database is almost better than single host installation with 3 nodes in ClusterControl..
author: Cihat Okan A.
pros: In the first install, it takes harder than console management for MAriaDB cluster for our 3 database servers. But after using its features, I saw that deploy & install zero configuration was easier than using a database.. Inserting & removing nodes to active cluster is a perfect module.. Tools of clusters are better than ever, haProxy, tuning options and others are very comfortable and reportable with ClusterControl..
cons: Adding nodes and hosts are very easy but I couldn't remove hosts from the active cluster.. There were some errors occured and also I've lost my all databases due to wrong settings.. But as a god mess, I have backups and re-installed it from zero config to fix all..
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: CLusterControl is a mature and easy to use product for management of database clustering software.
description: I've been following SeveralNines a couple of years and along with that way, I discovered the maturity of their product ClusterControl. All kinds of companies find their way to optimal data, for example, companies which are operating several database types that all need attending to. For such companies, Cluster Control is a very interesting solution to consider as it automates a lot of important tasks. But also at larger companies like VidaXL with a few dozen database nodes, the automatic recovery features for clusters are a great enrichment for their database operations.
author: Martijn W.
pros: 1. Automatic recovery of database (replication) clusters. 2. In my opinion one of the biggest challenges is to have in-depth knowledge about all their different database platforms being used. The builtin intelligence, advisory and health checking features of ClusterControl helps to overcome a big chunk of that gap. 3. Ease of deployment, in less then 15 minutes we are able to get a MariaDB or Percona cluster running.
cons: Faster and further development of features for other database platforms besides MySQL and MongoDB would be encouraged by me. For example PostgreSQL features and maturity could be improved.

review_title: Very easy to deploy a cluster, also it facilitates administration and monitoring.
author: Michel B.
pros: The numerous templates that facilitates the deployment of servers, the configuration of cluster, proxies, keepalive service and also the planning of backups. It is the little brother of Oracle Enterprise Manager software that I did used before for oracle databases
cons: I have no cons on the technical side. I find only the price per node a little high, it should be great to have a better price for qualification and development servers ans also standalone ones.
